verses.text: David declares, 'This is the house of the LORD God, and this is the altar' — fixing the temple's site at Ornan's threshingfloor, the place of atonement. Where judgment met mercy through sacrifice, God's house would rise. True worship is founded on the ground of atonement.

David gathers workmen and lays up gold, silver, brass, and timber 'in abundance' for the temple Solomon will build, saying the house must be 'exceeding magnifical.' He prepares lavishly for a work he will not finish. Faithful servants labour for a harvest others will reap.

David gathers workmen and lays up gold, silver, brass, and timber 'in abundance' for the temple Solomon will build, saying the house must be 'exceeding magnifical.' He prepares lavishly for a work he will not finish. Faithful servants labour for a harvest others will reap.

David charges Solomon, explaining he could not build because he was a man of war and blood, but Solomon, 'a man of rest,' will — and God will establish his throne. The temple belongs to the prince of peace, foreshadowing Christ, who builds the true house.

David blesses Solomon: 'the LORD be with thee, and prosper thou... be strong, and of good courage; dread not.' Keep God's law, and you will prosper. The cure for fear in any great task is the presence of God and obedience to His word.

David lists the vast stores he has prepared — gold and silver beyond weighing — and says, 'Arise therefore, and be doing, and the LORD be with thee.' Faith does not sit idle. Provision is gathered, but the work is done by rising and doing in God's strength.

David charges the princes to help Solomon: 'set your heart and your soul to seek the LORD your God,' and build the sanctuary. The great refrain of Chronicles sounds again. Those who would build for God must first set their whole hearts to seek Him.
